div.p: Do the Buddha's relics "reorganize" when the next Buddha comes — what texts support it, and is it likely?
dhātu-antaradhāna = the 5th of the five "disappearances." Commentarial & chronicle material — not canonical sutta.
Relics vanish to close Gotama's era; Metteyya arises much later. Sequential — not "because the next Buddha comes."
Not everyone in the group felt the "warbly" sensation.
Variation ⇒ paṭicca-samuppāda (conditionality): same object, different minds, different results. Buddhānussati is sufficient; adhiṭṭhāna only explains why this object.
Hold the prophecy lightly. Let a relic strengthen saddhā / saṃvega, then turn that toward investigating anicca in your own body. The relic points past itself.
adhiṭṭhāna The Buddha's resolve makes the relic a uniquely potent condition.
